skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Guides AI agents to create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces for websites, dashboards, components, and web visual design.
skill.summary: Build polished web interfaces with deliberate visual direction, responsive structure, and accessible details.
skill.author: YYH211

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and entropy indicators in SKILL.md. Context review found Markdown prose, CSS and JSX examples, HTML tag names, and an inline SVG data URI, with no executable script, secret handling, prompt injection, or remote exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Code Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown code fences, inline CSS property names, JSX examples, and HTML tag names. They are instructional frontend examples, not shell execution or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown and frontend syntax examples. No file contains runnable Ruby code, shell execution, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline SVG Data URI Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The URL-like value is a CSS data URI containing an embedded SVG noise texture. It does not contact a remote host or transmit user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 278 uses background-image with a data:image/svg+xml URI. This is local embedded asset data, not a network request.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Text Heuristics Flagged as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and entropy indicators are caused by prose, metadata, CSS terms, non-English text, and visual design examples. No cryptographic code, system probing, binary payload, or obfuscated instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain frontmatter, headings, CSS layout words, and styling examples. There is no semantic evidence of weak crypto, reconnaissance, or hidden payloads.

content.value_statement: Generic AI interfaces often look repetitive and unfinished. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward polished frontend work with clear visual direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides design thinking before implementation begins.
Suggests typography, color, motion, layout, and visual detail choices.
Supports websites, landing pages, dashboards, components, and web visual materials.
Encourages responsive design, accessibility checks, and semantic HTML.
Provides frontend examples for CSS, JSX motion components, and visual effects.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include runnable automation scripts.
It does not guarantee brand compliance without user-provided brand rules.
It may need adaptation for strict design systems or enterprise UI libraries.
It references external design resources but does not fetch or verify them.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Landing Page
use_cases.description: Turn a product concept into a distinctive responsive page with strong typography, layout, and conversion-focused sections.

use_cases.title: Improve an Internal Dashboard
use_cases.description: Refresh a data-heavy interface with clearer visual hierarchy, usable spacing, accessible contrast, and focused interaction details.

use_cases.title: Build Custom Web Components
use_cases.description: Design reusable buttons, cards, panels, and states that avoid default framework styling and feel intentional.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Page Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when starting a simple website or landing page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design and build a responsive landing page for my product. Choose a strong visual direction and explain the key design choices before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Component Refresh
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when an existing UI looks generic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign this component so it feels more distinctive and production ready. Improve typography, spacing, states, motion, and accessibility.

prompt_templates.title: Dashboard Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for dense operational or analytics screens.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished dashboard interface for these workflows. Prioritize scanning, comparison, responsive layout, and clear data hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Design System Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when defining a reusable visual language.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a frontend design direction for a small component system. Define typography, color, spacing, motion, states, and usage rules.

output_examples.input: A SaaS landing page for a workflow automation product.
output_examples.output: A responsive page concept with a bold hero, asymmetric section layout, focused calls to action, and accessible visual hierarchy.

output_examples.input: A dark analytics dashboard that feels too plain.
output_examples.output: A refined dashboard direction with denser information grouping, clearer cards, restrained motion, and stronger chart contrast.

output_examples.input: A set of buttons for a custom component library.
output_examples.output: A button system covering primary, secondary, text, loading, disabled, hover, and focus states with consistent visual rules.

best_practices: Provide the product goal, target users, and technical stack before requesting implementation.
Ask for accessibility, responsive behavior, and interaction states in the same request.
Review generated designs against your brand rules and existing component patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic modern design without product context.
Do not accept visual polish that weakens readability or keyboard access.
Do not mix this guidance with strict design systems without naming the constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill run code or install packages?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guidance skill and does not contain executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it work with React, Vue, or plain HTML and CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance applies to common frontend stacks and can be adapted to the user request.

faq.question: Does it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It helps create direction and implementation details, but existing design system rules should take priority.

faq.question: Is it suitable for dashboards?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for data-heavy screens, hierarchy, spacing, and restrained interaction design.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. It mentions design resources, but the skill itself does not make network requests.

faq.question: What makes it different from a basic UI prompt?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to choose a clear aesthetic direction and avoid repetitive AI-generated visual patterns.
